summary: When multiple substantial candidate boundaries exist for the same Item, prefer the latest candidate iff it is >3x larger than every earlier substantial candidate; otherwise return boundary_unverifiable.
reason: Modern SEC 10-Ks ship a TOC entry for Item X followed by the body for Item X; the v1 parser couldn't tell them apart and bailed. v1.1 heuristic preserves boundary safety while recovering the common case.
